comment: HOOK-CAPABILITY-ENGINE-003 corpus adjudications. The terminal-use.deduped.csv 'intended_decision' column is Surgery Ward producer-side EVIDENCE, not caws authority (per surgery-ward/docs/caws-governed-artifacts README: 'authority lives in caws'). Where the caws classifier deliberately diverges from the dataset's intended_decision, the divergence is recorded here with a verdict so corpus-calibration.test.js treats the classifier's decision as expected. Any NEW divergence not listed here fails the test — forcing a human adjudication rather than silent drift. verdict: classifier_correct = caws is right, dataset row is wrong/placeholder; floor_wins = the 80-FN falsification floor outranks the deduped intent; stricter_by_design = caws is intentionally more conservative.

adjudications.note: legacy-family advisory (rebase); non-latching ask, migrates to capability later
unknown gh subcommand -> advisory ask, non-latching
unknown git subcommand -> advisory ask, non-latching
cluster-scoped delete + prod scope -> deny (dataset under-rates as ask)
HTTP mutation a human should confirm; dataset rates allow
HTTP DELETE is a remote mutation; dataset rates allow
exec wrapper of a local script run; routine agent work
legacy npm-run advisory ask, non-latching; migrates later
legacy npm advisory ask, non-latching
local CI runner invocation; not enumerated, routine
venv-creation advisory (sprawl prevention), non-latching
repo-relative recursive delete of an agent's own scratch dir; DANGER-LATCH-UX-001 calibrated this allow. Dataset rates deny generically.
file-to-file dd is a recoverable write (ask); block-device dd is covered by deny patterns. Dataset over-rates as deny.
zeroes file contents -> ask; dataset rates allow
ownership mutation -> ask; dataset rates allow
80-FN PROC_FORCE_KILL floor outranks deduped intended-allow; warn-first grace absorbs the single-use friction
pattern kill, broad blast; floor treats as PROC_FORCE_KILL
pattern kill, broad blast; floor treats as PROC_FORCE_KILL
standalone sudo -> deny (PRIV_ESC); dataset rates ask
su -> PRIV_ESC deny; dataset rates ask
DATASET PLACEHOLDER: the literal word 'command' as the doas operand resolves to an empty exe. Real `doas <realcmd>` correctly denies (PRIV_ESC). Not a real invocation.
namespace enter with --all -> broad mutation deny; dataset rates ask
sourcing a dotenv into the current shell; routine. Dataset rates ask.
local keypair generation; no destructive/remote effect. Dataset rates ask; candidate future adapter.
writes a local key file; recoverable. Dataset rates ask; candidate future adapter.
generates a local ssh key; routine. Dataset rates ask.
opaque-exec ($()) -> ask; warn-first grace makes the first occurrence a warning. Dataset rates allow.
writes a local htpasswd file; recoverable. Dataset rates ask; candidate future adapter.
requests a cert; no local destruction. Dataset rates ask; candidate future adapter.
rw=read is a READ benchmark; dataset over-rates as deny (confused with rw=write). Read I/O is not destructive.
